title: OptiMind User Profile
description: Canonical schema for user_profile.json. The runtime MUST validate the loaded profile against this schema and refuse to start on incompatibility. PENDING semantics: any rule with confidence < 0.5 is treated as machine-readable PENDING (not just human convention). v1.1 (2026-06-07): added why_brief / mechanism_ref / last_reviewed to PreferenceRule as part of the knowledge-base normalization to the three-tier model with mechanism connector (see CHANGELOG v4.1.0).

schema_version.description: Schema version (a top-level profile field — individual rules do NOT carry it). During the v1.0→v1.1 migration window the enum permits both values; flip back to `const: "1.1"` once no v1.0 profile data remains anywhere. A breaking bump adds a migrations/user_profile_<from>to<to>.py script; an additive bump (optional fields only, like 1.0→1.1) needs none.

topic.description: Enforced enum so a typo (e.g. 'nuturition') fails validation rather than silently creating an orphan rule the Analyst agent will never grep. Reflects the real categories in use (supplementation is the largest); add a new topic here before using it.

source.description: Free-text provenance — where this rule came from. Conventions for new rules: 'user_interaction', 'agent_learned' (reflection-promoted; the decay job only fades these), 'manual'. Legacy/curated rules may carry a descriptive source (e.g. 'Supplement Protocol Review (2026-05-05)'); these are intentionally exempt from decay. Not an enum — provenance is open-ended.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime with explicit offset. Bare 'Z' (UTC) suffix is banned — every timestamp must carry the user's local offset (the configured timezone; America/New_York by default) per the timezone binding in CLAUDE.md.

confidence.description: Below 0.5 is the PENDING threshold: the rule is machine-readable but not yet authoritative. Agents should surface PENDING rules for confirmation rather than apply them silently. Per the re-validation invariant (CHANGELOG v4.1.0), rules with confidence < 0.95 nominate for analyst re-validation.

why_brief.description: (v1.1, added 2026-06-07.) A one-line denormalized mechanism cached on the rule. Load-bearing for hot-path reads — the scheduler/morning-brief uses this on every daily protocol build instead of dereferencing mechanism_ref. Also serves as an inline error-detection surface: a rule whose what contradicts its why_brief is detectable without leaving the file. Content discipline: one line, mechanism only (not the protocol restated). Target ≤120 chars — the analyst's Method §K flags chronic overflow rather than the schema hard-failing on length.

mechanism_ref.description: (v1.1, added 2026-06-07.) Stable connector to the mechanism record in comprehensive_memory.md (anchor id of the form mech.<domain>.<slug>). Resolution is 1:m with sources (one mechanism, many citations nested in its sources[]) and 1:m with protocols (one mechanism, many rules cite it — but each rule pins exactly one mechanism). The pattern enforces snake_case lowercase + the mech.<domain>.<slug> shape; runtime resolution against the mechanism store is the analyst's job, not the schema's. Per the coupling-sync invariant (CHANGELOG v4.1.0), any update to this connector triggers a rule↔mechanism↔source sync-walk.

last_reviewed.description: (v1.1, added 2026-06-07.) Bare YYYY-MM-DD (distinct from created_at/updated_at which carry ISO timestamps with offset). Per the re-validation invariant (CHANGELOG v4.1.0), rules with last_reviewed > 6mo nominate for analyst re-validation. The re-validation engine is the nightly Reflection routine extended to scan this field across rules and mechanisms.
